title: Should teens be allowed to carry handguns in Massachusetts?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court will review the legality of the handgun carry age limit of 21 and over.
Gun-rights advocates argue the age restriction violates the Second Amendment, referencing the 2022 Supreme Court ruling in Bruen.
The case involves Mikai Thomson, arrested at age 20 for illegal handgun possession, challenging the state's age-based carry law.

title: 5 takeaways from Don Sweeney’s Bruins press conference before trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins GM Don Sweeney plans strategic trade additions, focusing on long-term talent rather than short-term rentals before the deadline.
Sweeney emphasizes maintaining depth and not pursuing pure rental players, aiming to balance current needs with future growth.
Possible targets include players with long-term contracts, with Bruins willing to trade picks and prospects for significant impact players.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo set to return against Celtics while Jaylen Brown is out with an illness
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo expected to return for the Bucks against Celtics after missing 15 games due to calf strain.
Celtics' Jaylen Brown out with non-COVID illness; Neemias Queta also resting for Tuesday's game against the Bucks.
Celtics sit second in Eastern Conference; have split season series with Bucks, winning their last matchup in February.

title: Citing ‘wokeness,’ Pentagon cancels military fellowships at New England schools like MIT and Tufts
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: The Pentagon has canceled military fellowships at MIT, Tufts, and Brown, citing 'wokeness' and 'leftist ideology'.
Changes affect 93 fellowships across 22 institutions, including Yale and Middlebury, effective starting the 2026-27 academic year.
New potential fellowship partners include University of Michigan and Arizona State, chosen for 'intellectual freedom' criteria.

title: Rescued pocket-sized owl turning heads in Mass.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A northern saw-whet owl was rescued in Chelmsford, Massachusetts, after being found injured and unable to fly.
The small owl, about the size of a banana, has two broken bones and is being treated at Cape Ann Wildlife.
Newhouse Wildlife Rescue is raising funds for Cape Ann Wildlife, whose founder is battling lung cancer.

title: Massachusetts joins insurers cutting coverage for popular weight-loss drugs
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts Group Insurance Commission voted 10-7 to end GLP-1 drug coverage for state employees, citing budget strain.
Ending GLP-1 coverage affects 22,000 members, costing Massachusetts approximately $46 million annually in drug costs.
Governor Maura Healey promotes ending GLP-1 coverage to help save $100 million amid rising healthcare expenditures.

title: After Columbia incident, Healey and Campbell remind schools that ICE needs a judicial warrant to enter dorms
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Massachusetts officials remind schools ICE needs a judicial warrant to enter dorms after the Columbia University incident.
Attorney General Andrea Campbell issues guidance to educational institutions on protecting against warrantless ICE intrusions.
Governor Maura Healey enacts policies preventing ICE from using public property for enforcement actions without a warrant.

title: Lawsuit challenges National Park Service over content removal, including at a Mass. site
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A coalition filed a lawsuit in Boston challenging changes to National Park Service content under a Trump administration order.
The lawsuit cites removals and alterations of historical films and signs at Lowell National Historical Park and other New England sites.
Independent Massachusetts history institutions face financial and compliance pressures but retain control over their historical interpretations.

title: Mass. police dispatcher injured in Iran conflict
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A Sudbury police dispatcher serving with the U.S. Navy was injured in Tehran during missile strikes by Iran.
The unnamed 22-year-old was at a Dubai base when missiles landed within 50 yards, causing minor injuries.
Sudbury Police support their colleague as Massachusetts lawmakers criticize the U.S. military actions against Iran.

title: What Baylor Scheierman said about his fractured thumb
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Baylor Scheierman played 32 minutes against the 76ers, scoring 12 points, despite a fractured thumb.
The thumb injury occurred during a collision with Egor Demin in a Nets game on Friday night.
Scheierman previously broke the same thumb in high school and plans to continue playing with a splint.
